dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the situation has grown dire. {quest_ally} sent word that the {enemy_type} have doubled their assault. The defenders are holding, but barely.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The veterans are holding the line while the others fall back. {target_npc} tried to convince them to retreat together, but they refused: said {quest_stakes_the} {quest_stakes_is} bigger than any one of them. They need {quest_item} to shore up their position.
dialogueChunks.outro: Every hour they hold saves lives. If you can get {quest_item} to them, it might turn hours into days.

dialogueChunks.intro: One of the defenders broke. Ran in the night. Can't say I blame them, but now there's a gap that needs filling.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} caught the deserter {location_hint} and talked them down. They weren't a coward: they were a parent. {quest_ally} is trying to redistribute the remaining defenders, but without {quest_item}, {quest_threat_the} will pour through the gap.
dialogueChunks.outro: The ideal asks everything. Most people can give almost everything. That last fraction is where heroes are made, or broken.

dialogueChunks.intro: The person inside {quest_focus_the} is weakening. They've been exposed for days now, but they won't stop.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} says they need one more batch of {quest_item} to finish. After that, whether they survive depends on how much damage has already been done. They told {quest_ally}: 'The oath doesn't have an exception clause.'
dialogueChunks.outro: Find {quest_item} {location_hint} and bring it back. If they finish their work, {quest_stakes_the} {quest_stakes_is} saved. The only life in question is theirs.
